Package org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover

Class ReasonerDesc

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.ReasonerDesc
All Implemented Interfaces:
IReasonerDesc
public class ReasonerDesc extends Object implements IReasonerDesc
Bridge class used by the reasoner registry to store data about reasoners and provided to clients through its interface.

Implements lazy loading of reasoner instances.

Provides various facilities for reasoner version management.

      private static boolean containsWhitespaceOrColon(String str)

      Parameters:
      str - String to check for.

    • isContextDependent

    • makeUnknownReasonerDesc

      static ReasonerDesc makeUnknownReasonerDesc(String id)
      Returns a new ReasonerDesc with a name indicating the reasoner is unknown, and a dummy reasoner instance bearing the given reasoner id.

      This method is intended to be used only when the given id is not registered. Descriptor duplication must be avoided.

      Parameters:
      id - the id of the unknown reasoner.
      Returns:
      a new ReasonerDesc

    • makeUnknownReasonerDesc

      static ReasonerDesc makeUnknownReasonerDesc(IReasoner reasoner)
      Returns a new ReasonerDesc for the given reasoner, with a name indicating the reasoner is unknown.

      This method is intended to be used only when the given reasoner is not registered. Descriptor duplication must be avoided.

      Parameters:
      reasoner - the reasoner for which a descriptor is desired
      Returns:
      a new ReasonerDesc

    • copyWithVersion

      public ReasonerDesc copyWithVersion(int copyVersion)
      Returns a copy of this ReasonerDesc with the version modified according to the given parameter.
      Parameters:
      copyVersion - the desired version of the copy
      Returns:
      a new ReasonerDesc with the given version

    • getInstance

      public IReasoner getInstance()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns an instance of the reasoner.

      In case the reasoner has not been registered, or if there is a problem instantiating the reasoner class, a dummy reasoner instance is returned.

      A dummy reasoner cannot be replayed. An attempt to call the apply() method on it will always return an IReasonerFailure. One can know whether the returned instance is a dummy one by calling IReasonerRegistry.isDummyReasoner(IReasoner).

      Specified by:
      getInstance in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      an instance of the reasoner (might be a dummy one in case of error)

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns the id of the described reasoner.
      Specified by:
      getId in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      the id of the described reasoner

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns the name of the reasoner, as written in the extension point.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      the name of the reasoner

    • getVersion

      public int getVersion()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns the actual version of the described reasoner.

      The actual version can be different from the registered version (as returned by the reasoner instance) when the IReasonerDesc was obtained through proof deserialization, and the reasoner version has changed since the proof was serialized.

      Specified by:
      getVersion in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      a natural integer that is the actual version of the described reasoner, or NO_VERSION if the reasoner is not registered or not versioned
      See Also:
      • IReasonerDesc.hasVersionConflict()

    • getRegisteredVersion

      public int getRegisteredVersion()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns the registered version of the described reasoner.

      When the reasoner instance implements IVersionedReasoner, it is equivalent to calling getInstance().getVersion(). If the reasoner is not versioned or not registered, the method returns NO_VERSION.

      Specified by:
      getRegisteredVersion in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      the registered version of the reasoner, or NO_VERSION

    • getVersionedId

      public String getVersionedId()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns an encoding of both the id and the version of the described reasoner.

      The encoded version is the actual one, as returned by IReasonerDesc.getVersion(). If the reasoner is not versioned, then the bare id is returned.

      The result is intended to be used for reasoner persistence purposes.

      Specified by:
      getVersionedId in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      the String encoding of the id with the version

    • hasVersionConflict

      public boolean hasVersionConflict()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns whether the described reasoner has an actual version different from the registered version.
      Specified by:
      hasVersionConflict in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      true iff the actual version is different from the registered version.

    • isContextDependent

      public boolean isContextDependent()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns whether the described reasoner is context dependent.

      A reasoner is context dependent if its output depends on its external environment: language extensions, additional proof rules, or whatever may make it produce a different output at different times when applied with the same input.

      Context dependent reasoners are never reused directly based on proof dependencies alone. Instead, their output is checked in every proof where they appear, and for all applications inside each proof.

      Specified by:
      isContextDependent in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      true iff the reasoner is context dependent

    • isTrusted

      public boolean isTrusted()
      Description copied from interface: IReasonerDesc
      Returns whether the described reasoner is trusted.

      A reasoner is trusted if all of the following hold:

    • it is registered ( IReasonerRegistry.isRegistered(String))
    • it is NOT a dummy reasoner ( IReasonerRegistry.isDummyReasoner(IReasoner))
    • it has NO version conflict (IReasonerDesc.hasVersionConflict()

      • Specified by:
      isTrusted in interface IReasonerDesc
      Returns:
      true if the reasoner is trusted, false otherwise
